bankReconciliation.title: Bank Reconciliation
bankReconciliation.description: Reconcile bank statements with your book records
bankReconciliation.noReconciliations: No reconciliations yet. Start your first one above.
bankReconciliation.noItems: No items yet. Import a bank statement CSV to start.
bankReconciliation.noBookEntries: No unmatched book entries found for this period.
bankReconciliation.csvSampleHint: Download your bank statement as CSV from your bank's internet banking portal. Most banks support CSV or Excel export under Account Statement or Transaction History.
bankReconciliation.finalizeConfirm: Finalize this reconciliation? This will lock the period.
bankReconciliation.differenceNotZero: Difference must be zero to finalize. Current: {amount}
bankReconciliation.unmatchedHelpDesc: These bank transactions don't have matching records in your ERP. Common causes: bank charges, interest income, direct debits, or transactions not yet entered.
bankReconciliation.unmatchedStep1: Go to Journal Entries → New Entry and create a journal entry for each unmatched item (e.g., DR Bank Charges Expense, CR Bank Account for a bank fee)
bankReconciliation.unmatchedStep2: Come back here and click Auto-Match again — the new entries will match automatically
bankReconciliation.unmatchedStep3: Once all items are matched and Difference = 0, click Finalize Reconciliation

financialReports.title: Financial Reports
financialReports.description: Generate and view all financial statements, subsidiary books, and NGO-specific reports
financialReports.helpStep1Desc: Choose the fiscal year for which you want to generate reports. The current fiscal year is selected by default.
financialReports.helpStep2Desc: Click 'Generate' on any report card. The system will calculate the report from your journal entries and display a preview.
financialReports.helpStep3Desc: Review the generated data in the preview dialog. Reports are calculated in real-time from your accounting data.
financialReports.helpTip1: All reports are based on approved (posted) journal entries only. Draft entries are excluded.
financialReports.helpTip2: Core statements follow IPSAS/IFRS standards. NGO reports follow NGOAB and donor compliance requirements.
financialReports.helpTip3: For grant-specific or project-specific reports, use the donor and project report pages.

trial-balance.title: Trial Balance
trial-balance.desc: Summary of all ledger account balances — total debits must equal total credits.

chartOfAccounts.title: Chart of Accounts
chartOfAccounts.description: Manage your organization's hierarchical account structure for all financial transactions.
chartOfAccounts.step1Desc: The page shows all accounts in a collapsible tree structure. Click on group accounts to expand/collapse their children.
chartOfAccounts.step2Desc: Click 'Add Account' button. Fill in account code, name, type (Asset/Liability/Equity/Income/Expense), and optionally select a parent group account.
chartOfAccounts.step3Desc: Hover over any account row and click the pencil icon, or click on a leaf account. You can edit the name, description, fund code, and active status.
chartOfAccounts.step4Desc: Asset & Expense accounts have DEBIT nature. Liability, Income & Equity accounts have CREDIT nature. This is set automatically.
chartOfAccounts.step5Desc: Group accounts are headers that contain sub-accounts. Only detail (non-group) accounts can hold transactions.
chartOfAccounts.tip1: Account code and type cannot be changed after creation.
chartOfAccounts.tip2: Deactivating an account prevents new transactions but preserves history.
chartOfAccounts.tip3: Mark bank/cash accounts to use them in vouchers and reconciliation.

journalEntries.title: Journal Entries
journalEntries.description: Record and manage double-entry accounting transactions.
journalEntries.step1Desc: Browse all journal entries with their status (Draft/Approved). Click any row to view details or edit draft entries.
journalEntries.step2Desc: Click 'New Entry'. Fill in date, description, fiscal year, and add at least 2 lines. Total debits must equal total credits (double-entry).
journalEntries.step3Desc: Each line needs an account, description, and either a debit or credit amount. Click 'Add Line' for more rows.
journalEntries.step4Desc: Draft entries can be edited. Once posted (approved), the entry is locked and affects account balances and reports.
journalEntries.step5Desc: On the detail page, attach supporting documents (receipts, invoices, approval letters) using the file upload area.
journalEntries.tip1: Debits must always equal credits — the system enforces this.
journalEntries.tip2: Currency defaults to your organization's base currency but can be changed for foreign transactions.
journalEntries.tip3: Posted entries cannot be edited — create a reversing entry instead.

vouchers.title: Vouchers
vouchers.description: Create and manage payment and receipt vouchers. Each approved voucher auto-creates a journal entry.
vouchers.step1Desc: Browse all vouchers with type, amount, and status. Click any row to view details.
vouchers.step2Desc: Click 'New Voucher'. Select type (Debit/Receipt/Cash/Bank/Journal/Contra), enter date, description, and amount.
vouchers.step3Desc: Debit/Cash vouchers show a Payee field. Bank/Receipt vouchers show Bank Account and Cheque fields.
vouchers.step4Desc: On the detail page, click 'Approve'. This auto-creates a journal entry. Segregation of duty: approver must be different from preparer.
vouchers.step5Desc: Attach payment receipts, invoices, or bank advices as supporting documents.
vouchers.tip1: Approved vouchers auto-create journal entries — no manual JE needed.
vouchers.tip2: Bank vouchers require a bank account selection from Bank & Cash management.
vouchers.tip3: Project and Grant fields are optional but recommended for donor tracking.

bankReconciliation.title: Bank Reconciliation
bankReconciliation.description: Match your book records with bank statements to ensure all transactions are accounted for.
bankReconciliation.step1Desc: Choose the bank account you want to reconcile. Set the statement period (e.g., March 1–31). Enter the closing balance exactly as shown on your bank statement or passbook.
bankReconciliation.step2Desc: Click 'Import CSV'. Upload the CSV file downloaded from your bank. Map columns: which column is Date, Description, Debit (withdrawal), Credit (deposit). The system creates bank statement lines automatically.
bankReconciliation.step3Desc: Click 'Auto-Match'. The system compares each bank line with your journal entries by amount and date (within 3 days). Matched items turn green.
bankReconciliation.step4Desc: Unmatched bank items are usually: bank charges, interest income, or direct debits that haven't been recorded in the ERP yet. Create new journal entries for these.
bankReconciliation.step5Desc: When the difference between Book Balance and Statement Balance is zero (0.00), click 'Finalize'. This locks the period so no changes can be made — required for audit compliance.
bankReconciliation.exampleLine1: Your Sonali Bank account shows Book Balance = 8,200,000 BDT (from ERP).
bankReconciliation.exampleLine2: Your bank passbook/statement shows Closing Balance = 8,150,000 BDT.
bankReconciliation.exampleLine3: Enter 8,150,000 as Bank Statement Balance → Difference = 50,000 BDT.
bankReconciliation.exampleLine4: After importing CSV & auto-matching, you find 50,000 was a bank charge not recorded in ERP.
bankReconciliation.exampleLine5: Create a journal entry for the bank charge → Difference becomes 0 → Finalize!
bankReconciliation.tip1: The 'Bank Statement Balance' is the closing balance printed on your bank passbook or downloaded statement — not the ERP balance.
bankReconciliation.tip2: Reconcile monthly for each bank account — NGOAB and donor auditors require this.
bankReconciliation.tip3: Common unmatched items: bank charges, interest earned, standing orders, direct debits, and bounced cheques.

bankCash.title: Bank & Cash Management
bankCash.description: Manage all bank accounts, cash positions, and mobile banking accounts in one place.
bankCash.step1Desc: See all accounts with real-time balances. Use the filter tabs to filter by type. Summary cards show total balances at the top.
bankCash.step2Desc: Click 'Add Account'. Select type (Current/Savings/Fixed Deposit/Mobile Banking/Cash), enter account details.
bankCash.step3Desc: For the FC mother account registered with NGOAB, toggle 'NGOAB Mother Account' on. Required for all NGOs receiving foreign donations.
bankCash.step4Desc: Hover over any account row and click the pencil icon. You can update the name, bank details, status, and description.
bankCash.step5Desc: Add bKash, Nagad, or Rocket accounts as 'Mobile Banking' type. Used for beneficiary disbursements and field operations.
bankCash.tip1: Create bank accounts here before using them in vouchers or bank reconciliation.
bankCash.tip2: Cash accounts don't need bank details — just a name and opening balance.
bankCash.tip3: Deactivating an account hides it from dropdowns but preserves transaction history.
